## [00:00 - 03:01]

Hi everyone, my name is Craig Cannings and I am one of Jeff's team members and also one of the trainers in this program. I'm actually going to take you through a variety of practical quick start tutorials that you'll be able to take and quickly apply to your list building efforts. So we're going to kick it off with the topic Six Steps to Creating Your List Building System. So let's get started. Now as Jeff was mentioning in an earlier lesson, our company currently owns a private label version of a Weber called ProFollow. It has all the functions and features of a Weber, but Jeff has had such a great experience with this tool that he wanted to have a private label version of it for our company as well. And so when you're setting up your list building system, the two core elements that make it all come together is having your list host, your email provider like a Weber, and then having a squeeze page that you can integrate a web form from a Weber that will help you to build your list effectively. So what I want to do is I want to walk you through the six steps that you need to do to actually make this happen. And so step one is simply to create an email list and you do this within whatever platform you're using. The screenshot we have here is directly from ProFollow or a Weber and you simply would go down there, click, create a new list. And in the next tutorial, I'm actually going to demo how you would actually go and set up an email list within a Weber. But that's the very first thing you need to do is actually create your email list. Then step two, you need to go set up a web form within a Weber. What a web form does is it basically creates a script that you can then go insert and integrate into a squeeze page. It allows your email platform like a Weber to communicate with your squeeze page so that you're effectively adding names to an email list. So the web form is critical to making this whole process work. And in the next tutorial, I'll also show you how to set that up. We create the email list, we set up a web form. And then step three is to create an email verification list. And this has to be done if you're going to have a double opt-in email process. And Jeff obviously advised that and recommended that that would be the best route for you to go and would allow you to have probably the strongest relationship with the email providers and also the most qualified list. And so in order to do that, you need to set up an email verification message within a Weber. And it's a very easy process and I'll show you that in how to do that in the next tutorial. And so this is an example of what a verification message would look like. Obviously the person has to click on the link in order to receive the next email which would be your bribe, your offer, whatever you're providing to them. So that email verification message has to be put in place. And then step four, most importantly, you want to build a squeeze page. This is an example of a simple good looking squeeze page. And in one of the upcoming tutorials, I'm going to show you how to set up a squeeze

page using a WordPress theme called Optimize Press. There's certainly other tools you can use, but I'm going to show you an example of how to do it using Optimize Press. So once someone submits their email address, they'll come to a page like this and this is from a colleague of ours, Clay Collins, really good looking thank you page and he lays it out very simply. This is the email verification message that you need to set up in order to satisfy that double opt-in process.
